lines.en_lit: all her splendor” What is “All her splendor [hadara]”? Her chamber [ḥadra], You, what do you say He said to him: As I say, the Ark was buried in its place as it is stated: “And the staves were so long

Rabba said to Ulla: From where be inferred as it is written: “And they are there to this day,” And anywhere that it is written “to this day” it forever, But isn’t it written: “And the children of Benjamin did not drive out the Jebusites who inhabited Jerusalem; but the Jebusites dwelt with the children of Benjamin in Jerusalem, to this day” So too here, that were not exiled

But wasn’t it taught Rabbi Yehuda says: No person passed through Judea fifty-two years as it is stated: “I will raise crying and wailing for the mountains, and a lamentation for the pastures of the wilderness, for they have been burned, with no person passing through. And they do not hear the sound of the cattle; from the bird of the heavens to the beast [behema], all have fled and gone” Behema, has a numerical value of fifty-two,

And it was taught Rabbi Yosei says: seven years brimstone and salt endured in Eretz Yisrael, And Rabbi Yoḥanan said: What is the rationale of Rabbi Yosei; derived from “covenant” “covenant.” It is written here: “And he shall make a firm covenant with many for one week” And it is written there: They shall say: Because they forsook the covenant of the Lord, the God of their fathers”

said to him: Here, it is written: there”; there, it is not written. And anywhere that “there” is written it forever. raises an objection “And some of them, even of the sons of Simeon, five hundred men, went to Mount Seir having for their captains Pelatiah and Neariah and Rephaiah and Uzziel, the sons of Ishi. And they smote the remnant of the Amalekites who escaped, and dwelt there to this day”

But Sennacherib, king of Assyria, already come, and he had scrambled all the lands, as it is stated “And I have removed the bounds of the peoples, and have robbed their treasures” a conclusive refutation

Rav Naḥman said taught And the Rabbis say Ark was buried in the Chamber of the Woodshed. Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: We, too, have learned an incident involving a certain priest who was occupied and he saw a floor different from the others. He came and informed his friend but was unable to finish his report before his soul departed And they knew definitively that the Ark was buried there,

What was he doing, Rabbi Ḥelbo said: He was occupied with his axe, The school of Rabbi Yishmael taught: Two blemished priests were sorting wormy wood when the axe of one of them dropped and fell there, And fire burst out and consumed

Rabbi Yehuda raised a contradiction. It is written: “The ends of the staves were seen,” and it is written “But they could not be seen without” How They were seen and not seen, This was also taught “The ends of the staves were seen”; might they did not move from their position the verse states: “And the staves were so long.” might they ripped through the curtain and emerged the verse states: “They could not be seen without.”
How pushed and protruded and stuck out against the curtain and appeared like the two breasts of a woman As it is stated: “My beloved is to me like a bundle of myrrh, that lies between my breasts”

Rav Ketina said: When the Jewish people would ascend for Festivals, roll up the curtain for them and show them the cherubs, which were clinging to one another, and say to them: See how you are beloved before God, like the love of a male and female.
Rav Ḥisda raised an objection: “But they shall not go in to see the sacred objects as they are being covered, and Rav Yehuda said Rav said: When the vessels were put into their containers
Rav Naḥman said analogous to a bride; as long as she is in her father’s house, she is modest of her husband. once she comes to her father-in-law’s house she is no longer modest of her husband.

Rav Ḥana bar Rav Ketina raised an objection an incident involving a certain priest who was occupied said to him: you are speaking she was divorced. and when divorced she returns to her original beloved
With what are we dealing If we say to First Temple, was there a curtain Rather, to Second Temple; were there cherubs Actually, to First Temple, and what is the curtain the curtain of the gates.

As Rabbi Zeira said Rav said: There were thirteen curtains in the Temple: Seven opposite, seven gates; two one at the entrance to the Sanctuary and one at the entrance to the Entrance Hall. Two within the partition, and two corresponding to them in the upper chamber.

Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov said: Actually, to Second Temple: and there were images of cherubs there, As it is written: “And he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved figures of cherubs and palm trees and open flowers, “And he overlaid them with gold fitted upon the graven work”
And it is written: “According to the space of each with loyot” What is “According to the space of each with loyot”? Rabba bar Rav Sheila said:
